<parameter=code>
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

</parameter>
</function>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so the middle green moved from cols 30-32 to cols 27-29 (LEFT by 3). It's now at rows 30-32, cols 27-29 with W at center (31, 28).
So the green CAN move, but only LEFT and RIGHT (horizontally), not UP or DOWN (vertically). That explains why UP didn't work earlier!
Now the green is at cols 27-29, which is in the corridor between the left room and the purple wall. The purple wall is at cols 33-38. To get to the right frame, I need to go around the wall.
But if the green can only move LEFT and RIGHT, not UP and DOWN, then I can't go around the wall! The wall blocks horizontal movement from cols 27-29 to cols 39+.
Wait, but maybe the green CAN move UP and DOWN, just not from its current position? Let me check if the green is blocked by the room boundaries. The left room is at rows 21-41, cols 7-24. The corridor is at rows 21-41, cols 25-32. The green is at rows 30-32, cols 27-29.
Maybe the green can only move horizontally within the corridor, but can move vertically if it's in a different area?
Actually, let me reconsider. Earlier, when the green was at the left room (cols 18-20), I moved it LEFT into the left frame. That was a horizontal move. I never tried moving it UP or DOWN from that position.
Let me try moving the green UP from its current position (rows 30-32, cols 27-29).

<parameter=code>

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
